claim_boundary: Figure-quality checks validate local files, registry bindings, nonblank pixels, and substantive (non-empty, parseable) source data artifacts.

figures.alt_text: Bar chart of proposed, evaluated, accepted, rejected, and deferred candidate counts.
figures.claim_boundary: Lifecycle counts describe bounded orchestration, not autonomous approval.
figures.label: fig:autoresearch_candidate_lifecycle

figures.alt_text: Flow diagram linking program, proposals, evaluation, ledgers, claims, manuscript, readiness, and review.
figures.claim_boundary: The workflow is file-backed and inspectable but not self-approving.
figures.label: fig:autoresearch_closure_flow

figures.alt_text: Process-chain diagram and bar chart summarizing checked, missing, and mismatched local artifact records.
figures.claim_boundary: Integrity checks are local SHA-256 observations and are not externally signed provenance.
figures.label: fig:autoresearch_integrity_chain

figures.alt_text: Matrix of local security controls mapped to framework-inspired evidence surfaces and status.
figures.claim_boundary: Controls are local research-artifact safeguards, not production security certification.
figures.label: fig:autoresearch_security_control_matrix

figures.alt_text: Horizontal bar chart showing configured stages, supported claims, and required artifacts.
figures.claim_boundary: Readiness artifacts summarize local validation only; publication approval is human.
figures.label: fig:autoresearch_stage_matrix

figures.alt_text: Horizontal interval chart for accepted-candidate accuracy and macro F1.
figures.claim_boundary: Bootstrap intervals summarize local test-set resampling only.
figures.label: fig:ml_bootstrap_intervals

figures.alt_text: Reliability curve with accepted-candidate confidence-bin counts.
figures.claim_boundary: Calibration values describe the fixed local split only.
figures.label: fig:ml_calibration_reliability

figures.alt_text: Two-panel chart of candidate top-rank frequency and mean rank under deterministic bootstrap resampling.
figures.claim_boundary: Rank stability describes local resampling behavior, not model-selection certainty.
figures.label: fig:ml_candidate_rank_stability

figures.alt_text: Lollipop chart comparing baseline and evaluated candidate accuracies with Wilson interval error bars.
figures.claim_boundary: Scores apply only to the fixed local subset and configured candidates.
figures.label: fig:ml_candidate_scores

figures.alt_text: Heatmap of per-class precision, recall, and F1 for the accepted candidate.
figures.claim_boundary: Class metrics diagnose this run only and are not full-dataset estimates.
figures.label: fig:ml_classification_metrics_heatmap

figures.alt_text: Scatter plot comparing parameter count and held-out accuracy.
figures.claim_boundary: The plot compares this bounded candidate set and does not infer a scaling law.
figures.label: fig:ml_complexity_accuracy

figures.alt_text: Ten-by-ten confusion matrix for the accepted candidate on the local test split.
figures.claim_boundary: Confusion counts diagnose this run only and do not imply full-dataset generalization.
figures.label: fig:ml_confusion_matrix

figures.alt_text: Horizontal bar chart of the most frequent non-diagonal confusion pairs.
figures.claim_boundary: Pair counts identify local error cases and are not a general taxonomy.
figures.label: fig:ml_confusion_pairs

figures.alt_text: Grouped bars comparing train and test accuracy and loss by candidate.
figures.claim_boundary: Gaps are local bounded-loop diagnostics, not convergence guarantees.
figures.label: fig:ml_generalization_gap

figures.alt_text: Line chart of held-out accuracy by epoch for each evaluated candidate.
figures.claim_boundary: Learning curves diagnose configured training only, not convergence in general.
figures.label: fig:ml_learning_curves

figures.alt_text: Two-by-two heatmap of baseline and accepted-candidate correctness on matched examples.
figures.claim_boundary: Matched comparison is limited to the fixed local test split.
figures.label: fig:ml_paired_correctness

figures.alt_text: Bar chart of accepted-candidate accuracy for each true digit class.
figures.claim_boundary: Per-class values diagnose this local split only and do not certify robustness.
figures.label: fig:ml_per_class_accuracy

figures.alt_text: Two histograms of accepted-candidate confidence and prediction margin by correctness.
figures.claim_boundary: Distributions are descriptive diagnostics for the fixed local test split.
figures.label: fig:ml_probability_margin_distribution

figures.alt_text: Paired horizontal bar charts of Brier score and negative log likelihood with the accepted candidate highlighted.
figures.claim_boundary: Probability-quality metrics compare the configured evaluated candidates only.
figures.label: fig:ml_probability_quality

figures.alt_text: Heatmap of candidate accuracy under identity, shift, and low-contrast transforms.
figures.claim_boundary: Deterministic perturbations are a smoke test and do not certify robustness.
figures.label: fig:ml_robustness_matrix

figures.alt_text: Line chart comparing coverage, selective accuracy, and overall accuracy by confidence threshold.
figures.claim_boundary: Selective accuracy describes thresholded predictions on the fixed local split only.
figures.label: fig:ml_selective_accuracy

figures.alt_text: Two-panel chart showing final versus best held-out accuracy and train-test accuracy gaps by candidate.
figures.claim_boundary: Training dynamics diagnose this configured deterministic run only.
figures.label: fig:ml_training_dynamics

figures.alt_text: Grouped bar chart showing train and test example counts for each digit class.
figures.claim_boundary: Class counts describe the local fixed fixture and are not population statistics.
figures.label: fig:mnist_class_balance

figures.alt_text: Grid of accepted-candidate misclassified test examples with true and predicted labels.
figures.claim_boundary: Examples are qualitative diagnostics for this run, not an error taxonomy.
figures.label: fig:mnist_error_examples

figures.alt_text: Two-row grid with one local handwritten digit example for each class zero through nine.
figures.claim_boundary: The sheet illustrates the local fixed subset and is not a statistical sample claim.
figures.label: fig:mnist_subset_contact_sheet
